clinical_history: Young adult male with palpitations.  The ECG shows which of the following?
diagnosis: e) The 8th and 9th beats show a classic Wolff-Parkinson-White pattern (WPW) with a short PR, wide QRS and delta waves.  The tall R in V1 here is due to pre-excitation via a left lateral wall bypass tract.  The WPW pattern may be intermittent due, for example, to a transient increase in vagal tone which slows conduction in the AV node, allowing bypass tract conduction to become more evident. (The Brugada pattern, in contrast, is associated with a right bundle branch-like morphology and ST elevations V1-V2; see www.crtia.be/)
